rationale: Map {1: 0, 0: 0} encoded with key 1 before key 0. Canonical order requires bytewise-ascending order of each key's own encoded form, so key 0 (encoded 0x00) must precede key 1 (encoded 0x01). Well-formed, but violates map-key ordering (P2/D3).
Keys text "a" (0x6161, 2 bytes) then int 100000 (0x1a000186a0, 5 bytes). A length-first (RFC 7049) sorter keeps this order, but pure bytewise on the encoded key requires int first (0x1a < 0x61).
Keys text "aa" (0x626161) then text "b" (0x6162). Comparing decoded content puts "aa" first, but bytewise on the encoded key requires "b" first (head 0x61 < 0x62).
Keys int -1 (0x20, major type 1) then int 0 (0x00, major type 0). A numeric sort puts -1 first, but bytewise requires 0 first (major-type-0 head 0x00 < 0x20).
Keys text "" (0x60, major type 3) then byte string h'' (0x40, major type 2). Bytewise requires the byte-string key first (head 0x40 < 0x60).
Keys array [2] (0x8102) then array [1] (0x8101). Bytewise requires [1] first (0x8101 < 0x8102).
